function.name: search_notes
function.description: Search the vault by any combination of content, tags, and creation date range. All parameters are optional, but pass at least one. You should combine filters in a single call rather than chaining narrow ones. For example: search_notes(query='attention', tags=['research'], date_from='2026-01-01', date_to='2026-01-31') returns research notes about attention from January 2026. Up to 10 notes come back, each with path, title, tags, created and modified dates, and folder.

query.description: Free text searched in note content and title.

tags.description: Lowercase tag names. All must match (AND logic).

date_from.description: Inclusive lower bound on creation date in YYYY-MM-DD.

date_to.description: Inclusive upper bound on creation date in YYYY-MM-DD. For a single day, pass the same value as date_from.

function.name: get_note
function.description: Fetch a single note's full content. Pass either path or title. Title is the filename without .md, and spaces or underscores both work.

title.description: For example Transformers or Self Attention.

function.name: get_related_notes
function.description: Walk the [[wiki-link]] graph from a source note. direction='incoming' returns backlinks, meaning notes that link to this one. Use it for questions like 'what links to X' or 'what references X'. direction='outgoing' returns the notes this one links to, useful for 'what does X reference' or 'what does X build on'. direction='both' returns the union and is the right choice for 'connected to X' or 'related to X'.

direction.description: Which side of the graph to traverse.

function.name: get_vault_overview
function.description: Vault stats and the five most recently modified notes. Only use this for open ended questions about the vault itself, not for normal searches.
